config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau orang yang lemah. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan keadilan bagi tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun saat hak individu dihormati, lalu kepentingan bersama dijalankan secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that can easily become an excuse to silence weaker people or ignore basic fairness. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why collective stability and public order are emphasized, and I do think they matter in real life, especially in crowded and fast-changing societies. But when "society first" is used too broadly, it can cover up abuses and make individual rights feel optional, which I cannot accept. The better standard is balance: protect the common good, but keep clear limits so individual dignity and rights are not sacrificed whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, penggusuran yang tidak adil, atau tekanan pada kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak individu supaya tidak berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang bisa melindungi keduanya dengan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should generally outrank the rights of the individual, because once that principle is accepted too broadly, it becomes very easy for power to excuse harm in the name of the collective. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value social stability, order, and shared responsibility, and those things are not empty ideals, but they are not enough to justify treating individual rights as secondary by default. I have seen how "for the greater good" can be used to silence criticism or ignore unfair treatment, so I am very cautious about any argument that gives society automatic priority. A better approach is to protect the common interest while making sure individual dignity and basic rights remain firm limits, not flexible permissions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, dalih “demi masyarakat” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan kritik, mengabaikan kelompok kecil, atau melegitimasi keputusan yang sebenarnya menguntungkan pihak kuat. Bagi saya, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak individu yang jelas supaya tidak berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan. Masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak pribadi dihormati, lalu kepentingan umum dijalankan dengan adil dan transparan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because that principle too easily turns into a tool for abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value social order, stability, and responsibility to the group, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or excuse unfair treatment. When individual rights are treated as flexible, the people with less power are usually the first to lose. A healthy society should protect the common interest, but only within clear limits that keep basic rights and dignity non-negotiable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
